delta.note: Skill dramatically improves quality (visual brief, thumbnail validation, structured variants, correct aspect ratios) at cost of ~3x more tokens and ~4x more time

analyst_observations: Word count assertion fails consistently for with_skill — prompts run 51-55 words instead of max 50. The skill says 30-50 but the model slightly overshoots.
Baseline fails on format (no ``` blocks, no 'Varianta' labels) because it has no structural guidance — the grading partially penalizes format differences vs. actual quality.
The skill uniquely adds: visual_brief extraction, VISUAL_REFERENCE.md tone mapping, thumbnail validation, structured A/B/C variant types, and user choice prompt — none of which baseline produces.
Token cost is ~3x higher with skill (60K vs 20K) because the skill requires reading 3 files (analysis, visual reference, skill itself) and performing multi-step workflow.
